Power management functions for accessing the backup domain and checking standby boot status.

# Purpose
This code provides functions to manage power-related features on an STM32F7 microcontroller. It includes functions to access the backup domain, check if the system booted from standby mode, and clear the standby flag. The function [`pwr_access_backup_domain`](<#pwr_access_backup_domain>) enables or disables access to the backup domain by modifying the `PWR->CR1` register. The function [`pwr_did_boot_from_standby`](<#pwr_did_boot_from_standby>) checks the `PWR->CSR1` register to determine if the system booted from standby mode. The function [`pwr_clear_boot_from_standby_flag`](<#pwr_clear_boot_from_standby_flag>) clears the standby flag by setting the appropriate bit in the `PWR->CR1` register.

The code includes headers for power management and peripheral configuration, indicating that it is part of a larger system managing hardware peripherals. It interacts directly with hardware registers, which suggests it is intended for low-level hardware control. The code is not an executable but a library intended to be used by other parts of a system to manage power states and configurations on the STM32F7 microcontroller.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `drivers/pwr.h`
- `drivers/periph_config.h`
- `stm32f7xx.h`


# Functions

---
### pwr\_access\_backup\_domain<!-- {{#callable:pwr_access_backup_domain}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/robert/boot/src/drivers/pwr.c#L23>)

Controls access to the backup domain by enabling or disabling it based on the input parameter.
- **Inputs**:
    - `enable_access`: A boolean value that determines whether to enable (true) or disable (false) access to the backup domain.
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Call [`periph_config_enable`](<../../../../tintin/boot/src/drivers/periph_config.h.md#periph_config_enable>) to enable peripheral configuration for power control.
    - Check if `enable_access` is true; if so, use `__atomic_or_fetch` to set the `PWR_CR1_DBP` bit in `PWR->CR1` to enable access to the backup domain.
    - If `enable_access` is false, use `__atomic_and_fetch` to clear the `PWR_CR1_DBP` bit in `PWR->CR1` to disable access to the backup domain.
    - Call [`periph_config_disable`](<../../../../tintin/boot/src/drivers/periph_config.h.md#periph_config_disable>) to disable peripheral configuration for power control.
- **Output**: No return value.
- **Functions Called**:
    - [`periph_config_enable`](<../../../../tintin/boot/src/drivers/periph_config.h.md#periph_config_enable>)
    - [`periph_config_disable`](<../../../../tintin/boot/src/drivers/periph_config.h.md#periph_config_disable>)


---
### pwr\_did\_boot\_from\_standby<!-- {{#callable:pwr_did_boot_from_standby}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/robert/boot/src/drivers/pwr.c#L34>)

Checks if the system booted from standby mode by examining the standby flag in the power control/status register.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Read the `CSR1` register from the `PWR` peripheral.
    - Perform a bitwise AND operation with the `PWR_CSR1_SBF` flag.
    - Check if the result is not equal to zero to determine if the standby flag is set.
    - Return `true` if the standby flag is set, otherwise return `false`.
- **Output**: A boolean value indicating whether the system booted from standby mode (`true` if it did, `false` otherwise).


---
### pwr\_clear\_boot\_from\_standby\_flag<!-- {{#callable:pwr_clear_boot_from_standby_flag}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../platform/robert/boot/src/drivers/pwr.c#L39>)

Clears the boot from standby flag in the power control register.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Logic and Control Flow**:
    - Sets the `CSBF` bit in the `CR1` register of the `PWR` peripheral to clear the boot from standby flag.
- **Output**: No output is returned.
